question: For the `Point` class (from Q7), overload the stream insertion operator `<<` to enable printing `Point` objects directly to `std::cout` in the format `(x, y)`. Make this an external friend function.
errors: The stream insertion operator `operator<<` passes the `Point` object by value (`Point p`) instead of by constant reference (`const Point& p`). This creates an unnecessary copy of the `Point` object for every print operation, leading to reduced efficiency, especially for larger objects.

The operator uses `std::endl` which not only inserts a newline character but also flushes the output buffer. Flushing the buffer is often an unnecessary overhead, especially in performance-critical applications or loops. Using `\n` instead is generally more efficient when an explicit flush is not strictly required.
